title: sim: run_cosim_fst leaks memory
body:
### Version

main

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

see #5020 tests/sat/grom.ys

### Expected Behavior

no leaks detected

### Actual Behavior

`run_cosim_fst passes` a callback lambda to reconstructAllAtTimes which throws when it runs out of FST, dodging the `free(scatterptr);` in `libs/fst/fstapi.cc`. These function calls happen in sequence with indentation signifying stack depth:

```
SimWorker::run_cosim_fst
    FstData::reconstructAllAtTimes(,,,lambda throws)
        FstData::callback = lambda throws
        fstReaderIterBlocks2(,reconstruct_clb_attimes,reconstruct_clb_varlen_attimes,,);
            calloc(scatterptr) etc
            reconstruct_clb_attimes
                reconstruct_callback_attimes
                    callback()
                        throw fst_end_of_data_exception
    catch(fst_end_of_data_exception)
```

It seems like we should use fstReaderSetLimitTimeRange instead of handling the end time by throwing in a callback

CC @mmicko
code_blocks:
SimWorker::run_cosim_fst
    FstData::reconstructAllAtTimes(,,,lambda throws)
        FstData::callback = lambda throws
        fstReaderIterBlocks2(,reconstruct_clb_attimes,reconstruct_clb_varlen_attimes,,);
            calloc(scatterptr) etc
            reconstruct_clb_attimes
                reconstruct_callback_attimes
                    callback()
                        throw fst_end_of_data_exception
    catch(fst_end_of_data_exception)
